text.title: survey-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for survey-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Leading questions that presuppose an answer ("Don't you think X is better?")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Biases responses toward the implied preferred answer; data cannot be used for unbiased inference.

failure-mode.description: Surveys with no screener questions at the front
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Off-target respondents contaminate the sample; results apply to an undefined population.

failure-mode.description: Only closed questions with no open-ended field
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Misses unknown motivations and verbatim language that reveals how respondents actually think about the problem.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confidence intervals are too wide for actionable decision-making; switch to qualitative interviews instead.

failure-mode.description: No pilot test before full distribution
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confusing questions, broken logic, and unexpected completion times only surface in the pilot; fixing post-field is impossible.

failure-mode.description: Agents use Likert-5 for everything without justifying scale choice per question
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scale choice affects response distribution; 5-point scales compress nuance for attitude items where 7-point is standard.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated demographics duplicate or omit segments tracked in user-personas.md
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mismatched demographic options prevent cross-study comparison; always pass the personas doc as context and require options to be a subset.

failure-mode.description: Agent summarizes open-ended fields without disclosing how many responses were actually read
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent corpus truncation produces hallucinated synthesis that looks like full-corpus analysis.
